ending: Sen leaves the temple-city with its spatial treasure, accompanied by Misty Peak and the newly transformed Glimmer of Night. He survives the horde's ambush and an intelligent devil's attacks, then uses a dangerous divine-qi elixir to remove the devilish corruption from his wounds. The horde disperses, but the party cannot find the red-eyed devil's body. Li Yi Nuo returns to the Vermilion Blade Sect with Sen's elixirs and his letter of introduction to Jaw-Long. Her master accepts the peace offering and ends the sect's pursuit of Judgment's Gale, although the three afflicted disciples and Li Yi Nuo's vengeance for Xia remain unresolved. Sen holds Laughing River's treasure until the fox teaches Falling Leaf to change form. While travelling onward with Glimmer, Sen rescues captives from a raiding camp and assumes responsibility for an orphaned village girl. The nascent-soul cultivator grants her temporary refuge. Sen, Glimmer, Falling Leaf, and the girl finish the volume together in that domain, while Sen's altered cultivation, Laughing River's delayed ascension, the fox massacre dispute, the missing devil, and the child's permanent future remain open.
in_short: Sen travels toward Falling Leaf but is drawn into Laughing River's attempt to recover a time-space treasure from a holy city surrounded by a devilish horde. Li Yi Nuo, sent by the Vermilion Blade Sect to capture Sen, becomes entangled in the expedition, while Laughing River's descendant Misty Peak tries to stop him. An involuntary heavenly-qi breakthrough pushes Sen into late core formation and leaves unexplained divine qi within his body. Sen, Misty Peak, and the spider Glimmer of Night enter the ruins, complete separate mandala trials, and obtain the treasure. Their escape becomes a brutal battle with the horde and an intelligent red-eyed devil. Sen survives severe wounds and purges devilish qi with an improvised elixir, but the devil's body is not found. Li Yi Nuo persuades her sect to end its pursuit. Sen and Glimmer then rescue survivors of a destroyed village, and Sen brings an orphaned girl to the nascent-soul cultivator's domain for temporary safety.

recaps.text: Previously: Sen became an independent spirit-and-body cultivator trained by several formidable teachers, including Feng Ming and the spear master Jaw-Long. He counts Kho as an uncle, studies alchemy within Caihong's circle of expertise, and has learned to distrust sects and other powerful institutions. Falling Leaf, a Ghost Panther who is family to him, remains unable to freely return to her original form and is staying in a powerful nascent-soul cultivator's domain. Sen also owes the ancient fox Laughing River a favor.

So far this book: Li Yi Nuo cares for three sect disciples trapped in unbearable mental and emotional torment. A hostile elder orders her to find the unknown cultivator responsible, but her master kills that elder and warns the rest of the leadership against sacrificing her. Elsewhere, Sen befriends a ship's crew and refuses to abandon them when a powerful sea spirit beast attacks. He kills it after a difficult battle, diverts a huge wave around the ship with an extraordinary water technique, and suffers both severe wounds and an unfamiliar exhaustion. After recovering, he strengthens the ship with formations and leaves its grateful captain with funds to expand his trade. At Elder Bo's cove, Sen learns that divine qi has changed his Five-Fold Body Transformation into an uncertain sixth stage. Their argument becomes a brief, one-sided fight that exposes a weakness in Sen's unarmed methods. Elder Bo then explains that divine qi has strengthened the link between Sen's body and mortal soul. The desperate exertion used to save the ship may have strained that soul, so Sen must avoid forcing the same power without any reliable way to measure the damage. He leaves the cove and resumes his journey toward Falling Leaf.

Li Yi Nuo tracks Sen and discovers that the feared technique-user is also the charitable cultivator called Judgment's Gale. Sen admits afflicting the three disciples after they attacked him, but refuses to return to a sect he expects will try to kill him. Li Yi Nuo attacks to fulfill her mission. Sen defeats her, returns her weapon, treats her injuries, and later hears that her deeper goal is vengeance for Xia, a friend killed by a rival dual cultivator. He refuses to train Li Yi Nuo because his hostility toward sects could distort that relationship, but gives her a confidential introduction to Jaw-Long and a message for Kho. Before they separate, the spirit fox Laughing River invokes a favor Sen owes him and forces Li Yi Nuo to accompany them into the wilds. He says they must recover an old treasure but withholds practical details. Sen expects manipulation and danger, while Li Yi Nuo gradually becomes more cooperative with him. Their destination proves to be a sacred temple-city surrounded by an enormous horde of ghosts and devilish creatures. Believing Laughing River wants him to steal from holy ruins and seeing no survivable route through the horde, Sen refuses to continue.

Laughing River admits that foxes cannot enter the temple-city and that he brought Li Yi Nuo partly to make Sen less willing to walk away. When both humans still try to leave, he reveals that he is dying. Laughing River says he gained a ninth tail and should have ascended long ago, but delayed until he lost the strength to complete the passage. A treasure with time-space affinity inside the ruins may restore his chance. Sen personally examines him and confirms widespread decay, though not how much time remains. Li Yi Nuo refuses to be sent away despite lacking battlefield experience, and Sen rejects Laughing River's suggestion that killing her would simplify matters. The group studies the horde and concludes that direct battle and ordinary stealth are both unworkable. Li Yi Nuo identifies the need for a distraction, and Sen designs a vast formation intended to bombard one side of the horde long enough to open a route. Escape remains a separate problem. While placing the first section of formation flags, Sen is approached by a black-robed stranger who can evade his senses. The stranger recognizes Laughing River as a nine-tailed liar and tells Sen to let him die.

The stranger is a fox infiltrator intent on preventing Laughing River from obtaining the treasure. Sen learns to counter the attacker's layered illusions, but the fox drugs him, wounds Laughing River, and abducts Li Yi Nuo. Laughing River identifies the attacker as his descendant Misty Peak. Sen declares that the murder attempt and abduction exceed the favor he owed, then sets new terms: Laughing River must investigate whether Falling Leaf can ever regain or freely change her panther form. As Sen prepares two formation routes and a remotely triggered escape option, Misty Peak confronts him and confirms Li Yi Nuo is alive. She claims Laughing River summoned the continent's fox clans to a gathering near the Mountains of Sorrow, where hundreds were massacred, including her father. Since Laughing River was believed to be the only survivor, she regards him as the betrayer. Sen refuses to accept inherited accusation as proof and points out that Misty Peak herself would demand a chance to answer such a charge. She agrees to provide her full account and hear Laughing River's response. The hostage gives her little leverage because Sen will not surrender his judgment or the mission merely to recover Li Yi Nuo.

Laughing River confirms that he called the fatal fox gathering but denies betraying anyone. He says he rescued about a dozen foxes and withdrew afterward from guilt and grief, leaving the origin of the accusation unresolved. After initially neglecting the search because he dislikes Li Yi Nuo, he captures Misty Peak and confronts her privately. Sen refuses to mediate their family and political dispute. While they talk, he practices his weapons and maintains that he will offer shelter but take no responsibility for fox affairs. His plan to slow his cultivation then fails abruptly when a massive flow of heavenly qi forces another core layer to form. Sen strengthens two internal qi structures and reforges his body, but the energy continues beyond his control. An inaccessible inner gate finally opens and drains the excess somewhere connected to his soul. No expected tribulation follows. Sen wakes with denser qi, a larger core and dantian, and thousands of divine-qi points embedded in his skin. Laughing River judges that he has jumped from middle to late core formation and should not have survived the process. Sen can hide the visible glow, but neither he nor the fox understands its purpose, and Sen cannot reopen the inner channel.

Sen tests the strength and speed gained from his forced advancement and finds that even ordinary movement now requires restraint. Near the ruins, he negotiates a limited peace with the local spider matriarch, who permits his work while her brood watches. Sen later saves one of those watchers from a fire eagle. The spider begins guarding his shelter, apparently accepting a personal debt despite fearing him. Misty Peak asks to join the ruins expedition. Sen agrees only if Laughing River permits it, she can independently cross the anti-fox barrier, and she accepts responsibility for her own survival. Laughing River gives permission, while Misty Peak releases Li Yi Nuo from captivity. Sen completes and tests paired fire and lightning formations meant to pull part of the horde away from their chosen approach. The spider watcher stays beside him throughout the work, and the wider brood's surveillance becomes less tense. Sen still expects a fight because the distraction cannot draw every enemy away. After warning Li Yi Nuo, Misty Peak, and Laughing River that further personal conflict will make him abandon the plan, he finishes the final adjustments. The attempt to enter the ruins is set for dawn.

At dawn, Sen activates the fire and lightning formations while Laughing River adds an illusory army. Li Yi Nuo remains behind, but the saved spider unexpectedly follows Sen and Misty Peak toward the thinning edge of the horde. A spirit tries to possess Sen, and the divine qi in his skin destroys it. Rather than unleash an attack that might damage the sacred city, Sen spreads Heaven's Shadow through part of the horde, driving enemies into terror and conflict with one another. He then uses divine qi, lightning, and wind to force open a route. Sen, Misty Peak, and the spider cross the protective barrier, where a bell-like effect ends the immediate pursuit and strips away Misty Peak's human disguise. The city within is intact yet deliberately empty, with homes and durable possessions left behind. Their objective remains the spatial treasure Laughing River needs, and the central temple appears the likeliest location. However, a planted marker proves that the party's seemingly direct movement is being redirected without any detectable illusion. From above, Sen sees that the city is much larger than it appeared and that their route traces a deliberate pattern. He identifies the entire city as a mandala that has trapped them.

The sun does not move inside the ruins, and every attempt to retreat, fly directly to the temple, or escape the mandala returns the group to its imposed route. As they proceed, each traveller enters a separate trial. Sen's endless mountain ascent forces him to confront his regrets and accept that compassion and ruthlessness are both genuine parts of him. At the temple he meets an ancient caretaker, who explains that the city's defenses were designed to test Laughing River's readiness to ascend. The caretaker gives Sen the spatial treasure. On another path, the spider meets a transcendent matriarch, who grants him a humanoid form and the name Glimmer of Night so he can communicate. She directs him to travel with Sen for centuries. Misty Peak reports a similar instruction from fox ancestors. The caretaker says the absent inhabitants will eventually return and that the surrounding horde is contained because destroying it would cause equivalent evil to emerge elsewhere. The three travellers complete the mandala, but no one can determine how much outside time has passed. At the barrier, Sen discovers his remote formation trigger no longer works. Glimmer instead learns to crack beast cores so they detonate when thrown. The group prepares these as distractions, and Sen crosses the barrier first to begin their escape.

Sen opens the escape with Heaven's Shadow, cracked beast cores, darkness, lightning, and divine qi. Misty Peak warns Laughing River while Glimmer joins the bombardment. The horde has anticipated their exit, and an intelligent red-eyed devil attacks Sen with a spear. It is stronger than him and repeatedly drives devilish qi into severe chest wounds. Sen destroys part of its weapon and removes part of one arm, but he is blinded, deafened in one ear, poisoned, and eventually impaled. As the horde closes around him, he feeds divine qi into a damaged Stormhawk core. The devil's strike triggers a violent ground-level storm resembling a tribulation, scattering the horde. Glimmer rescues the incapacitated Sen, and Misty Peak guides the retreat to the gale house. After resting, Sen improvises a divine-qi elixir to purge the devilish corruption. The treatment nearly kills him and damages the shelter, but it removes the hostile qi and repairs his injuries. Once recovered, Sen confirms Glimmer still intends to travel with him and allows Misty Peak to accompany Laughing River's route only. Sen and Laughing River search the battlefield but find no body belonging to the red-eyed devil, leaving its survival unresolved.

Li Yi Nuo leaves the group near the Vermilion Blade Sect and reports her defeat, capture, the ruins expedition, Sen's battle with the horde, and his survival of the divine-qi elixir. She delivers Sen's elixirs and his introduction to Jaw-Long. Recognizing Sen's connections to Jaw-Long and Caihong, her master accepts the peace offering and ends the sect's pursuit of Judgment's Gale. Sen later leaves Misty Peak and Laughing River near Falling Leaf's destination but holds the spatial treasure until the fox teaches Falling Leaf to change form. Travelling with Glimmer, Sen senses an urgent call through a qi imprint on a necklace he once made for a village girl. They find her village burned and follow the surviving captives to a remote encampment. Sen kills the armed attackers and their leader but spares some others, while Glimmer protects the prisoners. They free the villagers and return recovered wealth to a surviving elder. The girl repeatedly leaves a resettlement village to find Sen, so he accepts immediate responsibility for her. Sen and Glimmer carry her through the wilds to the nascent-soul cultivator's domain. The cultivator grants her temporary refuge alongside Sen, Glimmer, and Falling Leaf, while Sen continues seeking a permanent home for her.
